_purpose: Approximate-dates narrator — tests uncertainty handling, timeline precision, review routing, no fake precision on fuzzy chronology

parents.occupation: Farm laborer and later mill worker
parents.notableLifeEvents: Moved the family north when Walter was young, maybe around 1944 or 1945.
Remembered family dates better than anyone else, but records were incomplete.

grandparents.memorableStories: Worked land he did not own and taught younger family members how to mend almost anything.
Known for hymn singing and herbal home remedies.

siblings.uniqueCharacteristics: Remembered as lively and quick with stories
siblings.sharedExperiences: Shared the family move north in childhood
siblings.memories: Walter was never certain of her exact birth year.

spouse.narrative: Married in the early 1960s, likely 1962 or 1963, in a small church ceremony remembered differently by family members.

children.narrative: One of two children; family records preserve only partial chronology.
Childhood and early adult years overlapped with several family moves.

marriage.weddingDetails: Small church wedding in the early 1960s; family later disagreed about the exact year.

familyTraditions.description: Sunday radio baseball and a large midday meal
Summer reunions after the family settled up north

earlyMemories.firstMemory: A red dirt road, a screen door, and summer heat.
earlyMemories.significantEvent: Moving north as a child, maybe during wartime or just after.

education.schooling: Left school young, likely after 8th or 9th grade.
education.careerProgression: Had steady work rather than one formal career, with several moves between states.
education.communityInvolvement: Union hall events and church men's breakfasts.

laterYears.retirement: Slowed down rather than formally retired all at once.
laterYears.lifeLessons: Dates matter less than who was there and what happened.
laterYears.adviceForFutureGenerations: Write things down because memory shifts.

hobbies.hobbies: Fishing, radio baseball, fixing things in the garage.
hobbies.worldEvents: Family memories are sometimes anchored to wartime and postwar movement, but dates remain approximate.
hobbies.personalChallenges: Periods of unstable work and repeated relocation.

health.lifestyleChanges: Walked more in later life after work became less physical.

technology.firstTechExperience: Color television felt like a major leap.

additionalNotes.messagesForFutureGenerations: Preserve uncertainty. Do not invent precision where records are incomplete. Exact birth date, migration year, wedding year, and children's birth chronology remain uncertain.
